info.title: Gray Whale API
info.description:
## Build Your Custom LIM

This API provides everything you need to launch an awesome, self-learning feed using Gray Whale AI's Large Interaction Models (LIMs). We've got you covered from onboarding to deployment and everything in between.

Head over to the [Platform Documentation](https://docs.graywhale.ai) to learn more. 

 ## WebSocket Feed 

This version of the Gray Whale API also supports a real-time, personalized feed via WebSocket. Scroll to the bottom of this page for the full WebSocket reference, message schemas, code examples, and an interactive live console. **If you want to use the WebSocket feed, you will need to train your model using the endpoint in this API.**

example.project_summary: Fly Finder is an AI-powered recommendation system that helps anglers discover the perfect fly-fishing flies based on their unique fishing style, target species, and local conditions. This self-learning feed evolves with each interaction, refining recommendations based on user preferences—whether they favor dry flies, nymphs, streamers, or emergers. By analyzing behavior, such as flies previously used, water conditions, and fishing locations, Fly Finder dynamically suggests the most effective patterns, materials, and techniques. Whether you're fishing for trout in mountain streams, bass in warm water, or steelhead in the Pacific Northwest, this AI-driven system ensures you're always casting with the best fly for the job.

example.title: Adams Dry Fly
example.description: A classic dry fly pattern that imitates a wide range of mayflies. Known for its versatility, it's effective in various water conditions and is a staple in any angler's fly box.

policies.policy: Someone browsing for dry flies is highly likely to be interested in other dry flies. While they may be interested in targeting other species, they are most likely pursuing trout and should be recommended other trout dry flies.

Source of a policy.

Used to distinguish between AI-generated and manually-created policies.

basicAuth.description: Username and Password provided by DevCamp (only required for project creation)

post.description:
Create a new Gray Whale project.

The `project_name` will serve as a unique identifier for the project throughout the Gray Whale system. Any project names that conflict with existing projects will be rejected.

Request Body:
- `project_name` (str): A unique name for the project. This will be used as an identifier for all subsequent interactions related to your project.
- `project_summary` (str): A detailed description of the project. This should include the project's goals, features, and any other relevant information. The more information you provide, the better your model will perform.
- `hacker_email` (str): An email address where notifications will be sent once your model is ready.
- `first_name` (Optional[str]): The first name of the project owner.
- `last_name` (Optional[str]): The last name of the project owner.

Returns:
- SecretPlatformProjectModel: An object containing the project details and access token needed for further development.

get.description:
Retrieve details of a specific Gray Whale project.

Parameters:
- `project_name` (str): The unique name of the project to retrieve.

Returns:
- PlatformProjectModel: An object containing the project's name, summary, and owner's email.

Send the project's access token to the owner's email address.

Parameters:
- `project_name` (str): The unique name of the project.

Returns:
- A confirmation message that the access token has been sent.

put.description:
Update an existing Gray Whale project.

Parameters:
- `project_name` (str): The unique name of the project to update.

Request Body (all fields optional):
- `project_summary` (str): Updated project description.
- `owner_email` (str): Updated owner email address.
- `first_name` (str): Updated first name.
- `last_name` (str): Updated last name.

Returns:
- PlatformProjectModel: The updated project details.

get.description:
Retrieve a paginated list of items in the project's catalog.

Parameters:
- `project_name` (str): The unique name of the project.
- `offset` (int, query, optional): Number of items to skip (default: 0).
- `limit` (int, query, optional): Maximum number of items to return (default: 50).

Returns:
- A list of GrayWhaleItem objects.

Retrieve a single item by its ID.

Parameters:
- `project_name` (str): The unique name of the project.
- `item_id` (str): The ID of the item to retrieve.

Returns:
- GrayWhaleItem: The requested item.

post.description:
Create one or more items in the project's catalog.

Parameters:
- `project_name` (str): The unique name of the project.

Request Body:
- A list of GrayWhaleItem objects to create.

Returns:
- The list of created GrayWhaleItem objects.

put.description:
Update an existing item in the project's catalog.

Parameters:
- `project_name` (str): The unique name of the project.
- `item_id` (str): The ID of the item to update.

Request Body:
- GrayWhaleItemUpdate: Fields to update (all optional).

Returns:
- GrayWhaleItem: The updated item.

delete.description:
Delete an item from the project's catalog.

Parameters:
- `project_name` (str): The unique name of the project.
- `item_id` (str): The ID of the item to delete.

Returns:
- A confirmation message.

get.description:
Retrieve all policies for the project's model.

Parameters:
- `project_name` (str): The unique name of the project.

Returns:
- A list of GrayWhaleModelPolicy objects.

post.description:
Create one or more policies for the project's model.

Parameters:
- `project_name` (str): The unique name of the project.

Request Body:
- A list of GrayWhaleModelPolicySubmission objects.

Returns:
- The list of created GrayWhaleModelPolicy objects.

get.description:
Retrieve a single policy by its ID.

Parameters:
- `project_name` (str): The unique name of the project.
- `policy_id` (str): The ID of the policy to retrieve.

Returns:
- GrayWhaleModelPolicy: The requested policy.

put.description:
Update an existing policy.

Parameters:
- `project_name` (str): The unique name of the project.
- `policy_id` (str): The ID of the policy to update.

Request Body:
- GrayWhaleModelPolicySubmission: The updated policy content.

Returns:
- GrayWhaleModelPolicy: The updated policy.

delete.description:
Delete a policy.

Parameters:
- `project_name` (str): The unique name of the project.
- `policy_id` (str): The ID of the policy to delete.

Returns:
- A confirmation message.

put.description:
Enable or disable a policy.

Parameters:
- `project_name` (str): The unique name of the project.
- `policy_id` (str): The ID of the policy to enable or disable.
- `enabled` (bool, query): Set to `true` to enable or `false` to disable the policy.

Returns:
- GrayWhaleModelPolicy: The updated policy.

get.description:
Retrieve a list of models for a specific hackathon project.

Parameters:
- `project_name` (str): The unique name of the project.

Returns:
- List[GrayWhaleModel]: A list of models associated with the specified project.

Retrieve details of a specific model.

Parameters:
- `project_name` (str): The unique name of the project.
- `model_id` (str): The ID of the model to retrieve.

Returns:
- GrayWhaleModel: The model details including accuracy, status, and associated policies.

post.description:
Train a new model for the project. This will use the project's current items and policies to create a new model.

Parameters:
- `project_name` (str): The unique name of the project.

Returns:
- GrayWhaleModel: The newly created model with its training status.

Retrain an existing model for the project. This will use the project's current items and policies to retrain the specified model.

Parameters:
- `project_name` (str): The unique name of the project.
- `model_id` (str): The existing model ID to retrain from.

Returns:
- GrayWhaleModel: The retrained model with its training status.

Activate a trained model for use in the project's feed.

Parameters:
- `project_name` (str): The unique name of the project.
- `model_id` (str): The ID of the model to activate.

Returns:
- GrayWhaleModel: The activated model.

Fetch the next group of item identifiers for a self-learning session.

Parameters:
- `project_name` (str): The name of the project for which the feed is requested.
- `session_id` (str): The identifier for the current user session.


Request Body:
- `page` (Optional[int]): The page number of the feed to be returned.
- `batch_count` (Optional[int]): The number of cards to be returned in the feed.
- `events` (Recommended[List[Dict]]): A list of events that have occurred in the session. The client skeleton handles all of the event submission logic - if you are interested in designing your own client we recommend looking at that reference. In addition to linger events, you can also submit abstract interest events which act as a thumbs up/down mechanism for influencing the next batch of recommendations.
- `search_prompt` (Optional[str]): A search prompt to be used to generate the feed. This acts as a top-level filter on the feed - searched items are prioritized over those inferred from the submitted events.


Returns:
    Dict: A dictionary containing a list of cards to be displayed in the feed. Refer to the client-side implementation for displaying these card types.

Fetch the next group of `Cards` for a self-learning session.

This endpoint serves as the primary delivery system for personalized recommendations. It utilizes the promoted model, session details, and user-provided inputs to generate the next batch of cards.

Parameters:
- `project_name` (str): The name of the project for which the feed is requested.
- `session_id` (str): The identifier for the current user session.


Request Body:
- `page` (Optional[int]): The page number of the feed to be returned.
- `batch_count` (Optional[int]): The number of cards to be returned in the feed.
- `events` (Recommended[List[Dict]]): A list of events that have occurred in the session. The client skeleton handles all of the event submission logic - if you are interested in designing your own client we recommend looking at that reference. In addition to linger events, you can also submit abstract interest events which act as a thumbs up/down mechanism for influencing the next batch of recommendations.
- `search_prompt` (Optional[str]): A search prompt to be used to generate the feed. This acts as a top-level filter on the feed - searched items are prioritized over those inferred from the submitted events.


Returns:
    Dict: A dictionary containing a list of cards to be displayed in the feed. Refer to the client-side implementation for displaying these card types.
